rulings.Case Title: MIQ LOGISTICS, LLC VS. SUNLINK CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award (Amended Petition)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 15, 2014, Line 1, RESPONDENT SUNLINK CORPORATION's Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award (Amended Petition). Denied. The Court has already considered and denied Respondent's Motion to Dismiss, or in the Alternative, Correct or Vacate Arbitration Award. On June 20, 2014, after hearing oral argument, the Court denied Respondent's original motion. Although Petitioner has filed an amended petition to correct a clerical error, the substance of the Petition and the Arbitration Award remain the same. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production Of Documents Requested Under Demand For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 15, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Requested Under Demand For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Production of Documents is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part, as follows: (1) RFP 1-2, 4-6: The attorney-client privilege and attorney work product objections are sustained, all other objections are overruled. Defendant Det Norske Veritas AS is a party. If it has relevant documents that were requested, it must produce them. An objection that it is not a proper party should be raised in a different motion. Defendant to provide code-compliant, verified responses and responsive documents within 14 days of this order. (2) RFP 3: All objections overruled. Defendant to provide code-compliant responses. Defendant to provide code-compliant, verified responses and responsive documents within 14 days of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BLUE TEE CORP. DBA BROWN-STRAUSS STEEL VS. DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES LTD; A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders Quashing Deposition Subpoenas For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents And Things Directed To Access Solar, Inc. And Cabazon Development Corp. By Plaintiff Blue Tee Corp.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 15, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES LTD; Motion For Orders Quashing Deposition Subpoenas For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents And Things Directed To Access Solar, Inc. And Cabazon Development Corp. By Plaintiff Blue Tee Corp. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Blue Tee v. Diversified Product Industries, LTD ("DPI"), et al. DPI's Motion to Quash Subpoenas to Access Solar, Inc. ("Access") and Cabazon Development Corp. ("Cabazon") are GRANTED. The fact that (1) Jack C. Pryor is listed on the CSLB website as the licensed contractor for DPI, Access, and Cabazon; (2) Access' website content references DPI; (3) DPI, Access, and Cabaon share an insurance policy; (4) a Work Order Estimate identifies Access for the work site and DPI as the billing entity; and (5) DPI was denied credit for a solar lift do not show good cause for the subpoenas. Information as to whether "DPI's business was intentionally slowed in order for its resources to be used to capitalize Access" (Opp, 6:8-9) can be obtained through written discovery directly from DPI. The requests for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN HENNESSY VS. YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 15, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is overruled. To state a claim for a violation of Labor Code section 970, Plaintiff must allege that a person has knowingly made false representations that "directly or indirectly¿influence, persuade, or engage any person to change from one place to another in this State¿or from any place within the State to any place outside, for the purpose of working in any branch of labor . ¿" Here, Plaintiff has alleged that Defendant (his employer) made false representations of employment in Georgia, and that, in reliance on Defendant's misrepresentations, Plaintiff sold his house in California, moved to temporary housing, forewent enrolling his children in school in California, enrolled his children in school in Georgia, entered into a contract for the purchase of a home in Georgia, and moved to Georgia. The fact that Plaintiff did not physically complete the move to Georgia until after he was informed that he was terminated from employment is not dispositive of whether Defendant's misrepresentations "directly or indirectly¿influence[d], persuade[d] or engage[d]" Plaintiff to relocate as required by the statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
